items.title: 70yo Perth man 'duped' into importing heroin to Sydney in fold-out bed jailed

children.content: Barry James Calverley's lawyers argued he was an innocent drug mule who naively and gullibly trusted a story about why he needed to bring the camping bed to Australia.

gallery.altText: Man in striped polo speaks to two uniformed officers

thumbnail.altText: Man in striped polo speaks to two uniformed officers

items.title: Long-awaited return of Perth-Bunbury train service announced

image.altText: The new Australind Train in storage in Perth.

children.content: A long-awaited train service between WA's two largest cities is set to get back on track in June after a near three-year wait.

items.title: Passengers return to Perth Airport after police investigate unattended bag

children.content: Passengers are allowed back into Terminal 1 after an exclusion zone was established at the airport for roughly two hours.

image.altText: People gather outside a door at Perth Airport that says 'domestic arrivals'.

items.title: Family asks for privacy as they mourn 'tragic loss' of 15yo Jack

children.content: The family of 15-year-old Jack Avenell say they are "heartbroken by the tragic loss" after he drowned at a remote Kimberley location.

image.altText: Jack Avenell

items.title: Job cuts spark calls for WA's struggling coal miners to merge

children.content: As the fallout continues from Premier Coal's decision to cut up to 100 jobs from its Collie workforce, calls grow for it to be merged with Griffin Coal.

articles.title: Workers to be axed, production slowed at one of WA's last coal mines

children.content: Premier Coal confirms job losses at its Collie operation, with workers set to be informed of the details.

image.altText: A long shot of a coal mine with a large truck in shot.

gallery.altText: Pit workings at Premier Coal's mine near Collie.
The entry sign on the Coalfields Highway heading into Collie.

thumbnail.altText: Pit workings at Premier Coal's mine near Collie.
The entry sign on the Coalfields Highway heading into Collie.

items.title: Tim Picton's accused killer pleads guilty over separate assault

children.content: A man who allegedly killed Labor identity Tim Picton in Perth admits assaulting another man in Kalamunda just days earlier.

image.altText: Brodie Dewar leaves court surrounded by reporters holding microphones.

items.title: Fishing ban at Perth lake considered after swan death

children.content: A Perth council is considering changing its by-laws to ban fishing at a host of lakes and reserves.

image.altText: A sign that reads "Fishing at Herdsman Lake Regional Park is not permitted" it has 'DBCA' at the bottom of the sign.

items.title: 'Public health issue': Box jellyfish swarm spotted off WA coast

children.content: Almost 100 deadly box jellyfish have gathered off a port in WA's north. Scientists say they likely belong to an unnamed species.

image.altText: A box jellyfish in the water

gallery.altText: A jellyfish inside a glass jar in a dark room

thumbnail.altText: A jellyfish inside a glass jar in a dark room

items.title: Workers to be axed, production slowed at one of WA's last coal mines

children.content: Premier Coal is set to cut jobs and production at its Collie operation, citing slowing demand for coal-fired power as a key factor.

gallery.altText: A long shot of a coal mine with a large truck in shot.
A long shot of a coal mine.

thumbnail.altText: A long shot of a coal mine with a large truck in shot.
A long shot of a coal mine.

items.title: Australia ready for AUKUS subs as China threat looms, US commander says

children.content: The US's Indo-Pacific commander praises work to upgrade Australia's AUKUS base, but says he needs more naval firepower in the region as China rapidly expands its military.

image.altText: Admiral Samuel Paparo sits at a desk and speaks into a microphone with uniformed personnel behind him.

items.title: 'Terrifying' experience for WA family as storm tears roof off their home

children.content: A woman describes her "terrifying" ordeal after a powerful cold front which sounded like a "jet plane" tore the roof off her family home while she was inside it on Monday night.

image.altText: Letterbox reading '10' in the foreground with a gutted home in the background.

items.title: Rates bill shock forecast as councils struggle with debt

children.content: A report exposes struggling local governments across the state as bills continue to climb.

image.altText: A report on a wooden desk top is titled "Local Government 2025" with "OAG" in the top corner.

items.title: Judge condemns 'outrageous' school stabbing planned on Snapchat

children.content: Two boys are sentenced for their roles in the stabbing of a 15-year-old boy at a Perth high school, offending the prosecutor said was part of a "game" formulated online.

image.altText: A yellow Snapchat app logo on a black smart phone.

items.title: 'Outrageous mess': About 2,000 AI road safety camera fines withdrawn in WA

children.content: About 2,000 fines valued at $1.1 million issued as a result of AI-assisted road safety cameras in WA have been withdrawn, six months after the devices were introduced.

gallery.altText: A screenshot of a fine issues for a seatbelt issue

thumbnail.altText: A screenshot of a fine issues for a seatbelt issue

items.title: Pressure on Department of Justice after second woman dies at Perth prison

children.content: Calls are mounting for a "transparent" government review of WA's Bandyup Women's Prison after a second Aboriginal inmate dies inside the facility in less than five weeks.

image.altText: A sign out the front of Bandyup Women's Prison

items.title: 'Large crocodile' attacks man in WA's far north

children.content: A man in his 20s has been flown to a Perth hospital after he was attacked by a crocodile at a popular camping spot north of Broome.

image.altText: A remote traffic sign

items.title: Travel giant's $240m overcharging scandal balloons to claims of fake agreements

children.content: Brisbane-based Corporate Travel Management has detailed a laundry list of concerns about a ballooning scandal, including reliance on potentially fake documents.

image.altText: Corporate Travel Management website.

items.title: E-bike rider dies after colliding with public bus in Perth's north-east

children.content: A 50-year-old man dies at the scene after his e-bike is involved in a crash with a Transperth bus at an intersection in Swan View, with police appealing for witnesses.

image.altText: Blue and white tape across a road

items.title: Last chance for answers as police admit error in search for Nannup Four

children.content: The family of a mother and daughter who went missing, along with two others, in WA almost 20 years ago are pinning their hopes on a last-ditch effort to uncover the truth of what became of them.

gallery.altText: Two men, a woman and a child holding their hands in a prayer pose.

thumbnail.altText: Two men, a woman and a child holding their hands in a prayer pose.

items.title: 'Spectacularly ill-advised': Energy giants condemn gas tax

children.content: An inquiry into the taxation of gas resources has heard from proponents for a tax, who believe Australians are being robbed of their fair share of wealth from the nation's natural resources, and opponents, who argue touching tax settings would spook investors.

image.altText: A woman wearing glasses leans earnestly towards a microphone.

items.title: How three different households make mealtimes easier

children.content: From share houses to solo living, this is how three households manage the cooking differently.

image.altText: Gemma Plunkett preparing a meal in her light-filled apartment kitchen.

items.title: Netball Australia CEO steps down as sport prepares for new era

children.content: Stacey West will step down as CEO of Netball Australia at the end of this week, ending a two-and-a-half-year tenure that has reshaped the sport in this country.

image.altText: Sport administrator posing while holding a netball inher arms

items.title: Residents urged to stay in as burnt battery product may react with forecast rain

children.content: Emergency services say a fire at the Li-ion Energy site in Maddington in Perth's south-east  — the biggest blaze of its kind in WA's history — will be reviewed to inform future responses to lithium battery fires.

image.altText: Flames can be seen billowing from a building in an industrial area.

items.title: 'It's been horrible': Your experiences with family estrangement

children.content: Our recent stories about the grief of family estrangement resonated strongly. Here's how some Australians have been affected by it.

image.altText: An illustration of a woman sitting on a park bench looking update with her hands on her head

items.title: How young boys helped Australians wage guerilla war against Japan in Timor

children.content: Incredible bonds were formed between Australian commandos and Timorese boys during World War II. The White Australia policy meant they had to say goodbye when the Anzacs departed.

image.altText: A graphic shows a contemporary colour of a landscape in Timor with a black and white photo of a boy and soldier in the middle.

items.title: 101yo Bombing of Darwin veteran wants Australians to know what unfolded

children.content: At 101 years old, Bombing of Darwin survivor Terrance Byrne wants Australians to know exactly what unfolded during the World War II attack on Darwin — still the single largest foreign attack on Australian soil.

gallery.altText: An older man sitting in front of table of framed black and white pictures.
A black and white photo shows a huge crater in the ground, alongside three undamaged oil tanks located near the coastline.
An army crew operating a large searchlight, the uniformed men are looking towards and pointing up at the night sky.

thumbnail.altText: An older man sitting in front of table of framed black and white pictures.
A black and white photo shows a huge crater in the ground, alongside three undamaged oil tanks located near the coastline.
An army crew operating a large searchlight, the uniformed men are looking towards and pointing up at the night sky.
